reforms.description: The Canadian government plans to re-examine measures to stimulate business R&D, including the Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) investment tax credit, to better target support for innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By better targeting R&D tax credits, the reform aims to enhance business innovation and productivity, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to re-examine measures to stimulate business R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing R&D support mechanisms, aiming to improve their effectiveness in fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Measures to stimulate business R&D should be re-examined.

reforms.description: The Canadian government developed an innovation strategy in 2002, which included a wide-ranging consultation process and resulted in 18 priority recommendations for action to enhance the national innovation agenda.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to improve innovation performance, which is essential for economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was laid out and involved a national summit with stakeholders.
reforms.importance_rationale: The innovation strategy represents a structural change in how Canada approaches innovation, aiming to align various stakeholders towards a common goal of enhancing innovation performance.
reforms.source_quote: The previous government laid out an innovation strategy for Canada in 2002 and embarked on a wide-ranging consultation process with the aim of building a national agenda for change involving all stakeholders.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has articulated a strategy to increase public and private investments in knowledge infrastructure, aiming to improve R&D performance and ensure that more firms benefit from the commercial application of knowledge.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased investments in knowledge infrastructure are expected to enhance R&D performance, leading to greater innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been organized into four key areas with specific targets set for improvement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards enhancing R&D performance through increased investment, which is crucial for Canada's economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Goals: Vastly increase public and private investments in knowledge infrastructure to improve R&D performance and ensure that a growing number of firms benefit from the commercial application of knowledge.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has proposed a framework for evaluating and prioritizing proposals for major science investments, aiming to establish a more coordinated and strategic approach to science projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A coordinated framework for science investments is expected to enhance the effectiveness of public R&D spending, leading to better innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The government’s National Science Advisor proposed a framework for evaluating and prioritising proposals for major science investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards a more strategic and coordinated approach to science funding, which is essential for maximizing the impact of public investments in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: A groundswell of support has emerged within the research community for a more co-ordinated and strategic framework for major science projects.

reforms.description: The federal government provides the Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) Investment Tax Credit to encourage private sector R&D, particularly assisting small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes R&D investment by businesses, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government provides the SR&ED Investment Tax Credit to encourage the private sector to undertake scientific research and experimental development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SR&ED program is a significant tool for promoting R&D in the private sector, although it is an established program rather than a new reform.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government provides the Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) Investment Tax Credit, which has the objective of encouraging the private sector to undertake scientific research and experimental development in Canada.

reforms.description: The Canadian government established an Expert Panel on Commercialization to enhance the commercialization of new technologies and products, recommending the creation of a business-led Commercialization Partnership Board to improve public policy design.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the commercialization of research, the initiative aims to enhance the flow of innovative products to the market, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The previous government set up an Expert Panel on Commercialization in May 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the panel and its recommendations represent a significant step towards enhancing the commercialization of research, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The previous government set up an Expert Panel on Commercialization in May 2005 to advise it on how to ensure more new technologies and products reach the Canadian marketplace.

reforms.description: The Canadian government is encouraged to develop a clearly articulated and integrated national science and technology policy to better assess individual project proposals and enhance the innovation strategy.
reforms.package_name: National Science and Technology Policy Development
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A coherent national policy framework can streamline innovation efforts and improve the effectiveness of public investments in R&D.
reforms.status_evidence: Developing a clearly articulated and integrated national science and technology policy is recommended.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the strategic direction of Canada's innovation efforts, which is essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Developing a clearly articulated and integrated national science and technology policy and a priority-setting exercise for Canadian science, within which individual project proposals can then be assessed.

reforms.description: The Canadian government is considering redesigning the SR&ED tax credits to better target R&D in new firms and encourage incremental R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeting R&D incentives towards new firms can stimulate innovation and increase overall R&D spending in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The option of redesigning the SR&ED tax credits is being explored.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives aimed at improving their effectiveness in promoting R&D.
reforms.source_quote: Examining whether the efficiency of the SR&ED tax credits might be improved. This should include exploring the option of carefully redesigning them to target R&D in new firms and to encourage incremental spending on R&D.

reforms.description: Canada's Innovation Strategy aims to enhance the country's innovation performance by fostering collaboration between public and private sectors, improving the business environment, and increasing investment in research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting collaboration and investment in R&D, the strategy is expected to enhance productivity and innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was officially announced as part of the economic survey.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing innovation in Canada, addressing multiple facets of the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Canada’s Innovation Strategy aims to enhance the country’s innovation performance.

reforms.description: The SR&ED tax credits for business R&D expenditures are designed to encourage private sector investment in research and development by providing tax incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the cost of R&D for businesses, the tax credits stimulate investment in innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit program was highlighted in the economic survey as a key innovation policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax credits are a significant tool for encouraging R&D investment but are part of a broader set of innovation policies.
reforms.source_quote: The SR&ED tax credits for business R&D expenditures are designed to encourage private sector investment.

reforms.description: The Canadian government aims to foster innovation by creating more economically efficient framework conditions for enterprises, which includes re-examining tax breaks to encourage business R&D and emphasizing the development of general skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the business environment and encouraging R&D investment can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy to foster innovation was highlighted as a top priority.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards fostering innovation, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Foster innovation. More economically efficient framework conditions for enterprises would also stimulate innovation and should be the top priority in the innovation strategy.

reforms.description: The Canadian authorities announced the Paperwork Burden Reduction Initiative (PBRI), aimed at reducing the costs of paperwork and regulatory compliance for small businesses, facilitating their operations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing compliance costs, the initiative encourages small businesses to invest and grow, thereby enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was announced and is aimed at reducing regulatory compliance costs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to alleviate burdens on small businesses, which are crucial for job creation and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In 2005 the Canadian authorities announced the Paperwork Burden Reduction Initiative (PBRI), a public–private sector partnership aimed at reducing the costs of paperwork and regulatory compliance for small businesses.

reforms.description: The federal government announced its willingness to eliminate all oil and gas revenue from the equalisation formula, which is expected to foster further investment in the energy sector.
reforms.component_name: Elimination of Oil and Gas Revenue Claw-back
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By removing disincentives for provinces to invest in their energy resources, this reform is likely to enhance economic activity and investment in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has announced its willingness to eliminate all oil and gas revenue from the equalisation formula.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy that could lead to substantial investment in the energy sector, impacting economic growth and resource management.
reforms.source_quote: The new federal government has announced its willingness to eliminate all oil and gas revenue from the equalisation formula.
